title: cassinia leptocephala subsp. leptocephala
intro: Cassinia leptocephala subsp. leptocephala, also known as bushy cassinia or white cassinia, is a shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its white flowers and bushy habit. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Cassinia leptocephala subsp. leptocephala has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to ease congestion and reduce inflammation. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications such as ointments and creams.
